global_context.scene_description: A photorealistic outdoor scene of an old, weathered residential outdoor air conditioning condenser unit on the side of a Phoenix-style stucco home. The aging AC unit dominates the lower-right portion of the frame. The unit shows years of Arizona sun: faded paint, light surface rust at corners, dust caked into protective grille fins, slightly bent fan guard wire. Above and around the unit, intense Phoenix afternoon heat radiates visibly from the metal cabinet — heat shimmer rising off the top of the unit, distorting the air just above it. Behind the unit, a tan stucco wall, and beyond that a slice of pale sun-bleached sky with intense direct sunlight. Long harsh shadows stretch across a concrete equipment pad. Sunlight floods from the upper-right of the frame, creating dramatic glare and lens flare. Heat haze in the air softens the distance. Mood: this thing is about to be cooked. The summer pressure is already starting. Photorealistic, shot on a 50mm lens, slight grain, late afternoon golden light with strong directional sun.
global_context.time_of_day: late afternoon, peak summer pressure approaching
global_context.weather_atmosphere: intense Phoenix heat, dry, dusty, heat shimmer in air

lighting.direction: raking from right, casting long hard shadows
lighting.quality: harsh, glaring, high-contrast with lens flare

composition.focal_point: the aged AC unit and the heat shimmer rising off its top
